name: Hot and Sour Soup- Szechuan
recipeIngredient: 1 (3 inch) square bean curd, thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak wood ears and lily buds in 1 cup hot water for 15 minutes; remove woody stems from both; shred wood ears with a cleaver.
2. Slice pork into thin strips; chop strips into shreds; Combine marinade ingredients in a small bowl; add pork, mix well; let stand 15 minutes.
3. In a medium saucepan, combine chicken stock, 5 cups water, and salt; bring to a boil over high heat; reduce heat to medium; add mushrooms, water chestnuts, bamboo shoots, carrot, wood ears, and lily buds; simmer 3 to 4 minutes.
4. Add pork with marinade and bean curd to the saucepan; bring to a boil.
5. Add Worchestershire sauce and vinegar.
6. Dissolve cornstarch in 5 tablespoons water to make a paste; slowly stir into soup.
7. Cook over medium heat until soup thickens slightly; stir egg into soup.
8. Add black pepper, white pepper, and sesame oil.
9. Serve hot. Yield: 4 servings.
